repo_signals: L3 smoke for code.wasm: webassembly build target and toolchain, js to wasm interop and abi via wasm-bindgen, linear memory model, bundling and loading the module, browser vs wasi runtime, and debugging a wasm module
plain prose note with no repository or domain signal

retrieval_query: wasm webassembly build target wasm-bindgen wasi emscripten linear memory abi interop bundling browser runtime debugging module instantiate

validation_smoke.name: code.wasm L3 validation smoke
signals: build target matches the runtime (wasm32-unknown-unknown for browser vs wasm32-wasi for server)
JS<->WASM values cross through a generated typed binding layer, not hand-decoded pointers
memory ownership across the boundary is explicit with no double-free or leak
the module is fetched and instantiated with streaming and the right import object

diagnosis_smoke.name: code.wasm diagnosis smoke
signals: wrong build target so the module needs imports the runtime cannot supply
passing strings/structs as raw pointers and mis-decoding linear memory
holding a JS view of WASM memory across a grow, reading a detached/stale buffer
MIME type or bundler config breaking instantiateStreaming
no source maps/DWARF so the WASM trap is undebuggable
